title: Podman F31 CI: rootless login/logout tests fail
body:
Several tests in test/e2e/login ... fail due to various issues including trying to copy files as a regular user into the cert dir.  In this case, a separate rootdir in the test's tmp dir should be used along with --cert-dir option to the command.

The offending tests are going to be disabled for rootless so that CI on F31 can continue.

The tests that fail are:


comments.body:
Note this section of code is not correct for rootless testing: https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go#L43-L52

1. Assuming the files are labeled correctly, I don't see why reading them should cause any selinux related problems.  Might just be a matter of `chcon` on the files to force correct labels (if os != ubuntu).
2. If `setenforce` is required, it should not be attempted when `isRootless()`.

more notes:  WRT: https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/test/e2e/login_logout_test.go#L69 and related operations failing in rootless.

1. If podman automatically checks '$HOME/.config/containers/certs.d/' then using that when `isRootless()` instead of hard-coding the `/etc/...` one, could be easy fix.

2. Maybe we need a top-level config. option for directory where to find certs?  That would also help eliminate the need for a hard-coded path in the tests.
Opened a PR to handle this.  If/When this gets merged it should fix this issue.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Container/image has been merged. now we wait for it to be vendored into libpod.
